title: General Knowledge Challenge
description: Test your knowledge across a wide variety of topics including geography, literature, science, and world trivia. A perfect starting point for trivia lovers!

questions.text: Which country is home to the Great Barrier Reef?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, located in the Coral Sea, off the coast of Queensland, Australia.

questions.text: Who wrote the famous play 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare early in his career, about two young Italian star-crossed lovers.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital and most populous city of Japan, serving as the country's economic and cultural hub.

questions.text: Which gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb Carbon Dioxide (CO2) from the air, combining it with water and sunlight to make glucose and release oxygen.

questions.text: What is the smallest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Arctic Ocean is the smallest and shallowest of the world's five major oceans, surrounding the North Pole.

title: Web Development Basics
description: Assess your basic understanding of frontend web development. Includes questions on HTML structure, CSS styling rules, and basic JavaScript triggers.

questions.text: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for creating web pages.

questions.text: Which HTML element is used to insert a line break?
questions.explanation:
The
tag is an empty element, which means that it has no end tag. It inserts a single line break.

questions.text: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It describes how HTML elements are to be displayed on screen, paper, or in other media.

questions.text: Where is the correct place to insert a JavaScript tag in HTML?
questions.explanation: JavaScript code can be placed inside the <head> or the <body> section of an HTML document, or linked as an external script.

questions.text: Which property is used in CSS to change the background color of an element?
questions.explanation: The 'background-color' CSS property sets the background color of an element using color keywords, hex, RGB, or HSL values.

title: Science Trivia
description: Dive into the fascinating world of physical science, biology, and chemistry. Check your understanding of elements, human biology, and space physics.

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for Gold is 'Au', derived from the Latin word 'aurum', which means shining dawn.

questions.text: How many bones are there in a fully-grown adult human body?
questions.explanation: An adult human skeleton is made up of 206 bones, whereas infants are born with around 270 bones which fuse over time.

questions.text: What is the primary gas that makes up the atmosphere of Venus?
questions.explanation: Venus's atmosphere is extremely dense and consists of 96.5% Carbon Dioxide (CO2), creating a runaway greenhouse effect.

questions.text: Which element has the atomic number 1?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen (H) is the first element on the periodic table with an atomic number of 1, containing exactly one proton.

questions.text: What is the speed of light in a vacuum (approximate)?
questions.explanation: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second, usually rounded to 300,000 km/s.

title: World History Quiz
description: Travel back in time to test your memory of world wars, ancient civilizations, empires, and monumental events that shaped our modern societies.

questions.text: Who was the first Emperor of the Roman Empire?
questions.explanation: Augustus (formerly Octavian) became the first Emperor of Rome in 27 BC, initiating the Pax Romana period.

questions.text: In which year did the Titanic sink?
questions.explanation: The Titanic struck an iceberg and sank on its maiden voyage on April 15, 1912.

questions.text: Which ancient civilization built the Machu Picchu citadel?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu is a 15th-century Inca citadel located in the Eastern Cordillera of southern Peru.

questions.text: Who was the primary author of the United States Declaration of Independence?
questions.explanation: Thomas Jefferson draft-wrote the declaration between June 11 and June 28, 1776, highlighting natural rights.

title: Sports Trivia
description: Are you a sports enthusiast? Face this collection of questions testing your records memory in football, basketball, and olympic disciplines.

questions.text: How many players are on the field for one football (soccer) team during a match?
questions.explanation: A standard soccer match is played between two teams of 11 players each, including one goalkeeper.

questions.text: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002), more than any other nation.

questions.text: How high is a regulation NBA basketball hoop in feet?
questions.explanation: A regulation NBA basketball rim is suspended exactly 10 feet (3.05 meters) above the court floor.

questions.text: How often are the summer Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The summer and winter Olympic Games are held every 4 years, rotating on alternate even years since 1994.

title: Pop Culture Trivia
description: Test your knowledge of blockbuster movies, chart-topping music, viral trends, and legendary icons of the entertainment industry.

questions.text: Which movie won the Academy Award for Best Picture in 2024?
questions.explanation: Christopher Nolan's 'Oppenheimer' won the Best Picture award at the 96th Academy Awards in 2024.

questions.text: What is the name of the fictional wizarding school in the 'Harry Potter' series?
questions.explanation: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry is the British wizarding school located in the Highlands of Scotland.

questions.text: Who is often referred to as the 'King of Pop'?
questions.explanation: Michael Jackson is globally referred to as the 'King of Pop' due to his massive impact on music, dance, and fashion.

questions.text: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (unadjusted for inflation)?
questions.explanation: James Cameron's 'Avatar' (2009) remains the highest-grossing movie of all time, yielding over $2.9 billion worldwide.

title: Space & Astronomy
description: Look up to the stars and test your grasp of planetary orbits, stellar lifecycles, historic space flights, and cosmic physics.

questions.text: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our Solar System, with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets combined.

questions.text: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because iron minerals in its soil oxidize, or rust, causing it to look red.

questions.text: What is the name of the first human-made satellite launched into space?
questions.explanation: Sputnik 1 was launched by the Soviet Union on October 4, 1957, beginning the Space Age.

questions.text: What force keeps the planets in orbit around the sun?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the attractive force that acts between any two masses, keeping planets locked in orbits around the massive Sun.

title: JavaScript Essentials
description: Go beyond basic variables and loops. Test your command of key JavaScript constructs like closures, scoping, prototypes, and promise behaviors.

questions.text: What is the output of: console.log(typeof NaN);
questions.explanation: NaN stands for 'Not-a-Number', but its data type is officially a 'number' in JavaScript.

questions.text: Which keyword is used to declare block-scoped variables in modern JavaScript?
questions.explanation: Modern JavaScript uses 'let' and 'const' to declare variables that are constrained within block scoping ({...}), unlike 'var'.

questions.text: What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A method to close browser tabs
An inner function that has access to outer function scope
A way to lock objects from edits
questions.explanation: A closure is the combination of a function bundled together with references to its surrounding state (the lexical environment).

questions.text: Which method is used to serialize a JavaScript object into a JSON string?
questions.explanation: JSON.stringify() parses a JavaScript value or object, converting it into a structured JSON string representation.
